name: Nagios XI API
what_it_does: Enterprise IT infrastructure monitoring platform with REST API for managing hosts, services, acknowledgements, downtime, and reporting in organizations with legacy Nagios-based monitoring.
use_cases: Querying host and service status for NOC automation and alert triage agents
Scheduling downtime windows programmatically during deployment pipelines
Acknowledging alerts and adding comments from automated runbook execution
Extracting SLA availability reports for executive dashboard automation
Creating and managing monitoring configurations for new infrastructure
not_for: Organizations starting fresh monitoring infrastructure from scratch
Real-time metric analytics requiring sub-minute granularity
best_when: An agent automates NOC operations for large enterprises with existing Nagios XI deployments managing thousands of on-premises hosts.
avoid_when: You're building a new monitoring stack — Prometheus or Datadog are better starting points.

auth.notes: API key from user profile in Nagios XI. Key passed as query parameter (apikey=) or HTTP header. No scope system — key grants access based on user's Nagios permissions.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Nagios Core (open-source) is free but lacks REST API. XI requires license.
pricing.paid_tiers: Nagios XI: $1,995+ for 100 hosts; Enterprise from $5,995
pricing.notes: Nagios Core (open-source predecessor) lacks the REST API. Nagios XI commercial version required for REST API. Pricing is per-host and one-time license.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: API documentation is PDF-based and not always current. Error responses are inconsistent — some operations return HTML error pages rather than JSON. API is functional but not developer-friendly.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: API does not natively support idempotency. Creating duplicate downtimes or acknowledgements is possible and must be handled in agent logic.
known_agent_gotchas: API key passed as URL query parameter (risk of key exposure in server logs)
Some actions return inconsistent response formats — JSON vs HTML vs plain text
Nagios XI version upgrades can break API behavior — version pinning recommended
Large environments (10k+ hosts) require careful pagination to avoid timeouts

security_score_components.security_notes: API key in URL query parameter is a security antipattern (logged in web server access logs). No RBAC at API level. TLS optional depending on server config. Legacy codebase with older security practices.
